chunk_summary: Guidelines for early post-gastrostomy feeding and managing complications in children with neurodisability, emphasizing infection prevention, emergency response to tube dislodgement, and feed tolerance strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the protocol for initiating feeding post-gastrostomy in stable pediatric patients.
Recognize signs of peristomal infection and appropriate first-response actions.
Learn emergency management steps for accidental tube dislodgement in the early post-procedure period.
Identify factors affecting feed tolerance and strategies to optimize feeding.
key_facts: Feeds can begin within hours post-procedure using iso-osmolar formula, starting with conservative volumes.
Peristomal infection risk is reduced with single-dose peri-procedure antibiotic prophylaxis aligned to local policy.
Accidental tube dislodgement within 6–12 weeks is an emergency; blind replacement should be avoided.
Persistent pneumoperitoneum beyond 3 days requires exclusion of bowel injury.
Bolus feeding is preferred unless contraindicated by severe reflux, retching, or intolerance.
reference_quote: Single-dose peri-procedure antibiotic prophylaxis reduces peristomal infection risk; align the specific agent with local antimicrobial policy. (ESPGHAN Position Paper)
